lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads 23:00. We are five hours in.
Find a place where you can sit. Or lie down. The position doesn't matter as much as the stillness.
[pause] I want you to notice your breath. Not control it. Just notice.
In through the nose. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out through the mouth.
The wind is picking up outside. You may hear it. That's okay. You're inside.
The temperature is dropping. Caroline said it would. [pause] She was right.
Your body knows this. Your body is already warm enough. Your body remembers how to survive a cold night.
Breathe in. [pause] Four counts. [pause] Hold it.
I want you to think about the choice you made today. The choice to be here. Inside.
Not everyone made that choice. [pause] Some people are outside right now.
They made a different choice. [pause] That choice is not your responsibility.
Release. [pause] Let the air leave your body.
Feel the weight of the walls around you. They are holding you. They are doing their job.
Your door is locked. Your windows are secure. You checked them. [pause] Some of you checked them twice.
That's wisdom. That's not paranoia.
Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
I'm going to ask you to scan your body now. Starting at the crown of your head.
Is there tension there? In your jaw?
Many of you are clenching. Release it. Your teeth don't need to touch.
Move down. Your shoulders. [pause] They're up around your ears, aren't they?
Drop them. [pause] All the way down.
Your chest. Your heart is beating faster than it usually does at this hour. That's normal.
You're listening to a radio station that doesn't legally exist. You're awake while the city is remaking itself.
Your heart should be faster. [pause] Acknowledge it. Don't fight it.
Your stomach. Are you hungry? Nauseous? Both?
The Order teaches that the body keeps score. Your body knows what night this is.
Your legs. Your feet. [pause] Are they cold?
The wind is moving around your home. It's looking for cracks. [pause] It's not finding any.
You are sealed in. [pause] That was your choice too.
Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release slowly.
I want you to think about the people you're not forgiving tonight.
[pause] There are people outside. People you knew once. People you decided not to help.
That decision — that weight — you can set it down for twelve hours.
Not forever. [pause] Just until the sun comes up.
There is a woman on Atwood Avenue right now who is listening to this broadcast instead of looking at her phone. She's made a list of names. Bad decisions. [pause] Her decisions.
She can't change those decisions tonight. [pause] Neither can you.
Breathe. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
The silence between your breaths — that's the only silence you get tonight. [pause] Treasure it.
Outside, there is noise. There is violence. There is change happening to a city you love.
You chose to sit with the quiet instead. [pause] That choice is a form of witness.
ka-sheh-la-veth-mora [pause] — forgive me.
The Sisters taught us that some words don't translate. [pause] Some languages are older than breath.
In. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold. [pause] The clock reads 23:00 and we are five hours deep.
Seven more hours until the gates of the city open. [pause] Seven more hours of this.
You will make it. [pause] You are making it right now.
Your body is still warm. Your door is still locked. [pause] And you are still listening.
That is enough. [pause] For now, that is enough.
Breathe. [pause] Just breathe.
I'll be back at 03:00. [pause] The night is still young.

tickerItems.text: Williamson Street barricade holding strong at 22:45. Neighborhood watch reports zero breaches. Temperature dropping — forecast shift confirmed, cold front moving faster than predicted.
Madison-wide cumulative: 61 confirmed. East Side accounting for 34% of activity. Up 6% from this point in 2168.
"The ones outside are not your responsibility. The door is."
Wind gusts reaching 22 mph near the Beltline corridor. Debris risk increasing. Secure any loose exterior items NOW if you haven't already.
